aliases: Ginger Salad (ဂျင်းသုတ်)

english: Ginger Salad (ဂျင်းသုတ်)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Burmese ginger salad (Gyin Thoke). The broader 'salad food product' class (FOODON_03315615) is used as the closest defensible ancestor. A more specific class does not currently exist in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP for vegetable-based salad dishes is used as the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for Burmese ginger salad in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Burmese ginger salad or a sufficiently close prepared salad dish. SNOMED CT is not used here to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical side serving of Ginger Salad in Singapore is approximately 100 g, presented as a small plate alongside rice or noodle dishes.

nutrient_source.title: Ginger Salad (ဂျင်းသုတ်) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ginger Salad (ဂျင်းသုတ်), cr_id G00010613, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from the total fat profile of a typical ginger salad preparation using USDA FoodData Central reference data for peanut oil and sesame oil components, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water are not reported by HPB for this entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on mixed vegetable salad with oil dressing
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Burmese Ginger Salad (Gyin Thoke). GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the closest analogous food category: mixed vegetable salad with oil-based dressing, which has a GI in the range 40–50 per the University of Sydney GI Database. The low carbohydrate content (5.9 g/100g) and high fat content (44.54 g/100g) further suppress postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 100 g serving = GI × carbohydrate_g / 100 = 45 × 5.9 / 100 = 2.655, rounded to 2.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100 g — well above the 600 mg/100 g threshold; low potassium (64 mg/100 g) provides no meaningful offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Ginger Salad in its standard preparation; sodium should be reduced by replacing fish sauce with low-sodium alternatives and minimising dried shrimp.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Extremely high fat (44.54 g/100 g) and caloric density (430 kcal/100 g) with zero dietary fibre impair insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Ginger Salad only in small portions and request a reduced-oil preparation, as the high fat load and absence of dietary fibre create a metabolically unfavourable profile for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (44.54 g/100 g) with estimated saturated fat ~7 g/100 g, high sodium (810 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (26 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Ginger Salad due to its high saturated fat and sodium content; a modified version using minimal oil, no dried shrimp, and low-sodium fish sauce would substantially reduce cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100 g exceeds RA threshold; estimated saturated fat ~7 g/100 g exceeds 5 g gate; nightshade ingredients (chilli, tomato) present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid standard Ginger Salad due to high sodium, high saturated fat, and the presence of nightshade ingredients (chilli, tomato) that may exacerbate joint inflammation, despite the anti-inflammatory gingerols in ginger.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ginger Salad (ဂျင်းသုတ်, romanized: Gyin Thoke) is a traditional Burmese salad widely available in Singapore's Burmese and Southeast Asian food stalls. It is prepared by thinly slicing or julienning young ginger and tossing it with a dressing of fish sauce, lime juice, and oil, then garnishing with dried shrimp, roasted peanuts, sesame seeds, crispy fried garlic, sliced chilli, and tomato. The result is a pungent, tangy, slightly spicy salad with a crunchy texture from the peanuts and fried garlic, and a sharp, aromatic bite from the raw ginger. Nutritionally, the dish is calorie-dense at 430 kcal per 100 g, driven almost entirely by its very high fat content of 44.54 g/100 g from oil, peanuts, and sesame. Protein is low at 1.32 g/100 g and carbohydrate is modest at 5.9 g/100 g. Dietary fibre is reported as zero. Sodium is very high at 810 mg/100 g due to fish sauce and dried shrimp, making this dish unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis without significant modification. The glycemic index is estimated as low (~45) owing to the minimal carbohydrate content and high fat, but the overall caloric and sodium burden makes it a high-concern food for multiple chronic conditions. Ginger itself contributes gingerols and shogaols, bioactive compounds with documented anti-inflammatory and gastroprotective properties, though these benefits are outweighed by the pro-inflammatory fat and sodium load in the standard preparation.
common_user_queries: ဂျင်းသုတ် nutrition
Is ginger salad good for diabetes?
Is ginger salad high in sodium?
Can I eat ginger salad if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010613, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference data for the oil and nut components, clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, clearly documented.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB for this entry; this is treated as a confirmed zero per the HPB source, not a data gap. 2. saturated_fat_g (7.0 g/100 g) is estimated from the fat composition of typical ginger salad ingredients (peanut oil, sesame oil, peanuts) using USDA FoodData Central reference data, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this traditional Burmese ginger salad preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. is_halal set to false as standard preparation includes dried shrimp and fish sauce which may not be halal-certified; consumers should verify with the specific vendor. 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Gyin Thoke was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16.
